attempt: Transfer Ticket 42's lesson: finite template probes are useful, but neither cycle absence nor bounded acyclicity proves the target while growth coordinates remain unclosed. The useful object is either a parametric lift theorem with a well-founded measure, or a compatible escaping cycle that becomes a counterexample target.

bounded_result.discarded_shortcut: finite error-cone template rank without cutoff-growth coordinates
bounded_result.retained_target: Every sufficiently large even integer has a parametric major/minor-arc error template whose certified margin is forced positive by a well-founded measure.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a compatible exceptional even-integer cycle whose normalized margin stays nonpositive

obstruction: The template layer can contain cycles after finite coordinates are hidden. This blocks any proof that uses only the finite quotient, but it does not produce a counterexample until the cycle is lifted to a compatible infinite object.
candidate_theorem: Every sufficiently large even integer has a parametric major/minor-arc error template whose certified margin is forced positive by a well-founded measure.
next_experiment: Lift error-cone template cycles into explicit cutoff constraints and test whether the margin can stay nonpositive.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
